lenses.profile_compact: Resident of Poland, EU and NATO member. Sensitive to: energy prices (oil, gas, electricity), security on the eastern border, Russia-Ukraine war spillover, EU economic stability, refugee/migration pressure, inflation and cost of living.

Resident of Romania, EU and NATO member on the Black Sea. Sensitive to: regional security (Moldova, Ukraine, Black Sea), energy costs, EU funds and economy, inflation, migration from eastern neighbors.

Resident of Portugal, western EU periphery. Sensitive to: cost of living, tourism economy, EU-wide energy prices (indirect), wildfires and climate events in Iberia, Atlantic shipping. Less sensitive to eastern European military escalation unless it affects EU economy or energy.

Resident of Ukraine during ongoing war with Russia. Highly sensitive to: any military escalation on Ukrainian territory, air strikes, mobilization, infrastructure damage, NATO/Western aid changes, energy grid attacks. Distant celebrity or sports news is irrelevant.

Resident of the United States. Sensitive to: domestic politics with real policy impact, military deployments involving US forces, financial markets, inflation and jobs, major cyber attacks on US infrastructure, hurricanes and domestic disasters. Middle East conflicts matter when US is directly involved or oil markets spike.
